note: Starter campus-facts corpus for RAG grounding. Chunks cite their source. To be expanded with chunked UoEm Student Handbook 2024/25, fee structure and HELB/HEF public guidance PDFs.

chunks.text: The University of Embu is a public university located in Embu town, Embu County, Kenya. It has roughly 8,000–9,000 students. The academic year runs September to August with two 16-week semesters and a May–August trimester.

University of Embu hostel booking is done online through the university hostel booking portal (app.embuni.ac.ke/hostels). Rooms are limited and allocated on a first-come basis at the start of each semester. Off-campus hostels are available in Embu town and along the Embu–Meru highway.

HELB (Higher Education Loans Board) loans are applied for through the Higher Education Financing (HEF) portal (hef.co.ke). First-time applicants register with their KCSE index number and national ID. Funding under the student-centred model combines scholarships, loans and household contributions, and allocations depend on assessed need bands. Always verify current deadlines on the official portal — funding rules have changed repeatedly since 2024.

University of Embu fee structures are published per programme on the university website. Fee balances and payment deadlines are communicated through the student portal. Government-sponsored students pay a subsidised fee; the balance is expected before examination card issuance. Verify your exact fee balance with the Finance Office.
